topic: Validate and stress-test the merge design for folding deep-loop-runtime into deep-loop-workflows as system-deep-loop: structural layout, bidirectional path-coupling repair, the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow, reference migration across commands/agents/READMEs/advisor-corpus, and whether fallback-router.ts should be wired for real GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback.

normalized_topic: validate and stress test the merge design for folding deep loop runtime into deep loop workflows as system deep loop structural layout bidirectional path coupling repair the system spec kit tooling borrow reference migration across commands agents readmes advisor corpus and whether fallback router ts should be wired for real glm 5 2 to mimo v2 5 pro fallback

focus: Structural layout and bidirectional path-coupling repair
noveltyJustification: Mostly confirmed the candidate layout and path rules, while adding a concrete missed WORKSPACE_ROOT repair in render-command-contract.cjs.
keyQuestions: Is the proposed system-deep-loop/runtime structural layout correct?
Are forward and reverse path-coupling repair rules correct at concrete call sites?
Is the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow repair correctly scoped and sufficient?
answeredQuestions: The runtime-as-infrastructure layout is correct and should not become a workflow mode.
The asymmetric path rule is correct, with one added root-depth repair for render-command-contract.cjs.
The system-spec-kit tooling-borrow is load-bearing and belongs in the structural phase.

keyFindings.summary: system-deep-loop/runtime should remain infrastructure, not a workflow mode.
Forward runtime-to-hub shared references keep hop-count and delete the old sibling segment.
render-command-contract.cjs has a WORKSPACE_ROOT depth constant that must gain one more parent hop after nesting.
Workflow packet code moves one hop closer to runtime and should target ../../runtime or ../../../runtime depending on nested depth.
Runtime typecheck and system-spec-kit council test discovery both rely on deep-loop-runtime paths and must be repaired in the structural phase.

ruledOut.approach: Add runtime as a workflow mode
ruledOut.reason: The live registry treats runtime as backendKind/runtimeLoopType infrastructure, while user-facing modes remain research/review/council/improvement.

recommendedNextFocus: Inventory external reference migration categories and stress-test child 003 coverage.

noveltyJustification: Confirmed most migration categories and added two refinements: fresh Stage-A baseline authority and explicit allowlisting for historical active eval fixtures.
keyQuestions: Is external reference migration complete and safely staged across commands, agents, READMEs, hooks, advisor corpus, and graph metadata?
answeredQuestions: External reference migration is broadly complete and safely staged if Stage A is treated as authoritative and historical eval fixtures are reviewed or allowlisted instead of blindly renamed.

keyFindings.summary: Live old-name inventory differs from the spec count; Stage A must capture the authoritative baseline immediately before edits.
Compiled /deep command contracts embed generated headers, source digests, and old paths, so they must be regenerated rather than hand-edited.
Advisor constants and routing fixtures require field-scoped migration plus accuracy re-baseline.
mk-deep-loop-guard's registry path and test fixture must update together.
Both .opencode and .claude agent trees carry real old references and must be edited in lockstep.
Active system-spec-kit eval fixtures intentionally contain historical old-name queries/decoys and should be reviewed or allowlisted, not blindly renamed.
Sibling graph metadata needs edge collapse from two old identities to one system-deep-loop identity.

ruledOut.approach: Blind repo-wide find/replace of old skill names
ruledOut.reason: Generated contracts, advisor fields, graph edges, and historical eval fixtures need different handling.

recommendedNextFocus: Evaluate fallback-router.ts and current fanout retry behavior.

noveltyJustification: Confirmed the zero-caller gap and added implementation blockers: narrow call-site assumption, missing registry edge, canonical-id/provider-slug mapping, approval policy, and fallback-label attribution.
keyQuestions: Should fallback-router.ts be wired now for GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback?
answeredQuestions: Fallback-router wiring should remain optional/deferred for the structural merge unless the operator explicitly expands scope to behavior changes.

keyFindings.summary: fallback-router.ts is a shipped pure decision primitive with test-only callers and no production dispatch integration.
fanout-pool retries the same item/index/label until maxRetries and then sets retry_exhausted; no model-swap path exists.
Child 004's proposed fanout-pool-only call site is too narrow because fanout-run owns model-bearing lineages, prompt construction, command dispatch, and artifact validation.
The current model profile registry lists both GLM-5.2 and MiMo-V2.5-Pro with fallback_target null, so GLM-to-MiMo fallback would be new policy/configuration.
Fallback router ids differ from executable provider slugs, so integration needs canonical-id to CLI-slug normalization.
The fallback router's approved-model guard means substitution must be explicitly approved by config/policy, not silently inferred from a global edge.
A fallback lineage needs a distinct label/artifact directory and status event to avoid mixing failed GLM retry state with MiMo replacement output.

ruledOut.approach: Make child 004 mandatory inside the structural merge without an operator scope-expansion decision
ruledOut.reason: Fallback wiring is behavior expansion requiring registry/schema, caller approval, model-id normalization, and attribution changes; it is not required for the rename/nesting/reference migration.

recommendedNextFocus: Run synthesis-readiness/convergence pass and produce final research synthesis if no contradictions appear.

noveltyJustification: No new contradictions; confirmed parent/child phase consistency and two existing amendment requirements before synthesis.
keyQuestions: Can this lineage legally synthesize after answering all five key questions and meeting the minimum iteration floor?
answeredQuestions: Yes. All five key questions are evidence-backed, the lineage completed four iterations, and parent/child specs are consistent with the recommended sequence and optional 004 deferral.

keyFindings.summary: Parent scope explicitly excludes fanout behavior changes and tracks fallback-router wiring as optional/operator-gated.
Child 002's path plan is directionally correct but should add render-command-contract.cjs WORKSPACE_ROOT depth repair before implementation.
Child 003's residual-grep stage needs a reviewed allowlist for semantically historical active eval fixtures.
Child 005 already treats 004 as conditional, so the recommended sequence is 001 -> 002 -> 003 -> 005 unless 004 is explicitly scoped in.
Deep-research stop contract permits convergence when all key questions are evidence-backed after the minimum floor.

ruledOut.reason: All key questions are answered, the minimum floor is met, and the convergence pass produced no new contradictions.

recommendedNextFocus: Synthesize final research.md and close lineage state.
